hct.source: Low hematocrit (percentage of red blood cells in the blood) is often observed in psoriasis patients, reflecting anemia.

hgb.source: Anemia (low hemoglobin) is common in psoriasis, particularly in severe cases, due to chronic inflammation and potential iron deficiency.

mch.source: Low mean corpuscular hemoglobin (MCH) can be seen in psoriasis, potentially indicating hypochromic anemia or iron deficiency.

mchc.source: Low mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration (MCHC) can be seen in psoriasis, potentially indicating hypochromic anemia or iron deficiency.

mcv.source: Low mean corpuscular volume (MCV) can be seen in psoriasis, potentially indicating microcytic anemia or iron deficiency.

plt.source: Elevated platelet count (thrombocytosis) is a common finding in psoriasis, possibly related to inflammation and increased platelet production.

rbc.source: Low red blood cell count (RBC) can be seen in psoriasis, particularly in severe cases, due to anemia.

rdw.source: Elevated RDW can indicate increased red blood cell production variability, which may be seen in psoriasis due to inflammation.

wbc.source: Elevated white blood cell count (leukocytosis) can be seen in psoriasis due to inflammation and immune activation.
